sections.paragraph:
m. ( Dan 9:27, שְׁבֻעַ זאֹת Gen 29:27, should be rendered the week of this woman), const. שְׁבוּעַ Gen 29:27, 28 Gen 29:28dual שְׁבֻעַיִם Levit. 12:5 pl. שָׁבֻעִים m. ( Dan 9:25 10:2 , Dan 10:3), and שָׁבֻעֹות, const. שְׁבֻעֹות, with suff. שָׁבֻעֹותֵיכֶם Num 28:26, a hebdomad, ἑβδομάς, septenary number (denom. from שֶׁבַע seven, compare עָשֹֹׂור a decad).

(1) of days, a week, Gen 29:27, 28 Gen 29:28. Dan 10:2, שְׁלשָׁה שָׁבֻעִים יָמִים “through three weeks” (where יָמִים is not a genit., see יָמִים No. 2, , page 342, A ). חַג שָׁבֻעֹות the feast of (seven) weeks, pentecost, so called from the seven weeks which were counted from the passover to this festival, Deu 16:9. Fully, Tob_2:1 , ἁγία ἑπτὰ ἑβδομάδων. But, Eze 45:21, חַג שְׁבֻעֹות יָמִים the feast of hebdomads of days is the passover, which was celebrated through the whole of seven days.

(2) a hebdomad of years, Dan 9:24, seqq. Compare Hebdomas annorum, Gell. N. A. iii. 10.
